description:
Goal: make macro availability and recursive expansion an ordinary source-tier fact workflow, including require, imported macros, local macros, macro executable readiness, and source-order `Fz.Compiler` service evaluation from fz-rh2.11.7.18.

Signal:
- fixtures/cross_module_macro runs through fz2: import Helpers, only: [twice: 1] waits on Helpers' surface, learns that twice/1 is a macro, waits for its MacroExecutable fact, and expands it in App's caller context
- explicit require Mod is handled by a source pre-scan: compiler2 waits on ModuleDefined(Mod), selects the requested macro set (only: or all macros minus except:), and then waits on those exact MacroExecutable facts
- local macro use observes source-order/availability rules; a reserved macro name is not enough until that local macro reaches executable readiness
- returned quoted AST recursively expands until it contains only compiler-service forms and runtime body syntax
- function definitions are not recorded as FunctionSource facts until their fully expanded AST reaches the `Fz.Compiler` service call; module indexing no longer saves raw/unexpanded function bodies
- recursive expansion tracks the stable quoted-source key {heap, root} plus an expansion depth/budget signal so runaway expansion diagnoses deterministically
- compiler-service forms produced during expansion are evaluated by source production through the .11.7.18 `Fz.Compiler` host-services boundary, threading the active `__ENV__`/scope session forward in source order
- telemetry/work-graph output shows provider waits as exact ModuleDefined plus selected MacroExecutable dependencies and shows expanded-source publication through source_publish

Change:
- pre-scan source forms for require and imported-macro demands before processing the rest of the module/source body
- wait on provider ModuleDefined facts to know the actual exported macro surface; do not guess symbol kind from syntax alone
- after surface definition, select the exact macro FunctionIds demanded by require / import only: / import except: and wait on their MacroExecutable readiness facts
- bind imported macro names only after provider surface and executable readiness are known; never bind a guessed function placeholder for a macro import
- implement macro lookup for local, imported, and required remote macros over namespace plus the scope snapshot from .11.7.3, not over a separate compile-env store
- run expansions with the .11.7.5 MacroExecutable runner and feed `__CALLER__` / `__ENV__` from the current source-production session
- continue expanding function forms before invoking `Fz.Compiler.define`, so downstream function jobs receive the expanded FunctionSource root and do not need to know about item macros
- evaluate any returned compiler-service forms through the .11.7.18 host-services boundary immediately, before continuing the source walk
- feed returned source roots back through the same source-production service/readers after validating source ownership and preserving {heap, root} identity
- reject ambiguous, unavailable, or recursively runaway macro calls with diagnostics at source-production time

Why:
- cross-module macros are where the pre-expanded input assumption breaks most obviously
- require is a compile-order and surface-availability problem, not ambient mutable env state
- the existing import wait pattern already proves the right shape: wait on provider facts, then bind what is actually exported
- Elixir's successful model is source-order expansion with caller env threaded into macro execution and compiler-service calls; compiler2 should compose source-production facts, macro executable facts, and `Fz.Compiler` services the same way rather than inventing a second macro compiler
- saving function AST before expansion recreates the old pre-expanded-input assumption at the exact boundary this arc is replacing
title: Compiler2 macro availability: pre-scan require/import, wait provider surface, and expand recursively
